title: Work Interest Constellation

introduction.subtitle: What kinds of work actually energize you? A six-axis interest map inspired by Holland's RIASEC model.
introduction.description:
This is not a career test that assigns job titles. It maps the types of activities that give you energy, so you can understand your work preferences in venture-building contexts. Original items, inspired by the RIASEC conceptual framework — not the official O*NET instrument.

Rate your genuine interest in each activity. Both broad and focused profiles are useful.

Use the result as language for reflection and conversation, not as a permanent label. The terms are meant to make patterns easier to notice, compare, and discuss.
introduction.privacyNote: Your answers stay in this browser. The result belongs to you. Sharing is your choice.
introduction.disclaimer: Inspired by Holland's RIASEC model and O*NET Interest Profiler concepts. Items are original. This is not the official O*NET instrument and not a validated vocational assessment.

questions.text: Assembling or repairing physical equipment.

conceptHelp.title: Builder: Assembling or repairing physical equipment.
conceptHelp.summary: Builder means interest in tangible, practical, physical, mechanical, or hands-on work. This statement is one small behavioral signal for that pattern: "Assembling or repairing physical equipment.".
conceptHelp.whyItMatters: Self-knowledge items are useful when they help you notice repeated behavior. They become misleading if one answer is treated as a permanent identity or diagnosis.
conceptHelp.beforeAnswering: Think across normal weeks, not one unusually good or bad day. Rate the statement by how often it describes your real preference or behavior.
conceptHelp.example: A high answer means this statement is regularly true for you; a low answer means it is rare, context-specific, or not how you usually operate.

attribution.text: Inspired by Holland's RIASEC model and O*NET Interest Profiler concepts. Items are original and not from the official O*NET instrument.

questionStageTitle: Learn the concept, then answer from evidence
questionStageDescription: Use the concept button when a term is unfamiliar. Prefer observable evidence over aspiration or ideal self-description.
